title: Indulgent and Shattering British Chwinamul Yorkshire-Pudding with Smoky Crust
description: This rich, citrusy, and fragrant British dish is a unique twist on traditional Yorkshire pudding, featuring chwinamul and a smoky crust.

ingredients.name: chwinamul
ingredients.notes: Ayurvedic-rajasic-friendly alternative to traditional Yorkshire pudding ingredient

ingredients.name: sunflower oil
ingredients.notes: for brushing the crust and adding smokiness

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, whisk together flour, eggs, whole milk, water, sunflower oil, cold-pressed rapeseed oil, citrus zest, sea salt, and smoked paprika until smooth.
Add the chopped chwinamul to the mixture and gently fold in.
Pour the mixture into a greased 12-cup muffin tin.
Bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until puffed and golden brown.
Remove from the oven and let cool in the tin for 5 minutes.
Transfer the Yorkshire puddings to a wire rack to cool completely.
To add the smoky crust, brush the tops of the Yorkshire puddings with sunflower oil and sprinkle with smoked paprika.
Return to the oven and bake for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until the crust is crispy and golden brown.
